title: Single-Nucleus RNA Sequencing from Fresh-Frozen Human Heart Tissue
author: Natalia-Del Pilar Vanegas

steps.title: Lysis Buffer
steps.instruction: Lysis Buffer. Use 1 mL per 50 mg tissue %s %s Wash / Resuspension Buffer %s %s 0.25 M Sucrose Buffer — dissolve 4.28 g sucrose in 50 mL WashBuffer; keep cold.

steps.title: Keep all reagents and samples at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Keep all reagents and samples at 4 °C. Use wide-bore tips at every pipetting step. Start with 50 mg of pulverized frozen tissue (cryopulverized under liquid nitrogen). Maintaining the tissue frozen until lysis is critical. Add 200 µL lysis buffer to the pulverized tissue. Homogenize with cordless pellet pestle motor for ~30 seconds. Avoid over-homogenization. Add remaining 800 µL lysis buffer (total 1 mL). Incubate on ice for exactly 1 minute. Prolonged lysis in the presence of IGEPAL and Digitonin risks nuclear lysis. Filter lysate through a 30 µm cell strainer to remove large myofibrillar debris and undigested tissue fragments, which are abundant in heart tissue. Vortex filtered lysate briefly (10 sec) to dissociate loose aggregates before centrifugation. Centrifuge at 500 × g, 3 min, 4 °C. Discard supernatant completely. Resuspend pellet in 1 mL wash buffer using gentle wide-bore pipetting (5–6 strokes). Centrifuge at 300 × g, 5 min, 4 °C. Discard supernatant. Repeat once (2 washes total). The lower g-force in wash steps favors nuclei recovery over debris pellet down. RNase Inhibitor in the wash buffer protects nuclear RNA throughout these steps. Resuspend pellet in 1 mL wash buffer. Add 10 µL DNase I (1 U/µL). Mix by inverting the tube 2–3× only — do not vortex, as this disrupts nuclei. Incubate on ice for 5 minutes. DNase I treatment is especially critical for heart tissue. Stop DNase I activity by adding 10 µL 50 mM EDTA. EDTA chelates the Mg²⁺ cofactor required for DNase I catalysis. Filter suspension through a 20 µm strainer to remove residual debris and any remainingaggregates not dissolved by DNase I treatment. Centrifuge at 300 × g, 5 min, 4 °C. Discard supernatant. Resuspend pellet in 1 mL 0.25 M sucrose buffer. Centrifuge at 500 × g, 5 min, 4 °C. The sucrose cushion provides density-based separation. Discard the top layer carefully. Retain the pellet. Resuspend nuclei in an appropriate volume of Wash Buffer. Assess nuclei by microscopy before counting: nuclei from heart tissue should appear round and uniform with minimal debris. If significant clumping persists, perform an additional DNase I treatment (5 min on ice) and re-filter through 20 µm. Count nuclei using Countess III FL with Propidium Iodide staining. Target: ≥ 500,000 intact nuclei at ≥ 70% viability for fixation input.

steps.title: Perform fixation according to Demonstrated Protocol...
steps.instruction: Perform fixation according to Demonstrated Protocol CG000478 (Fixation of Cells & Nuclei for Chromium Fixed RNA Profiling). Recount fixed nuclei using Countess II FL with Ethidium Homodimer-1 before proceeding.

steps.title: Prepare a Hybridization Mix for each...
steps.instruction: Prepare a Hybridization Mix for each sample by combining the fixed nuclei suspension with the sample-specific Probe Barcode set (LHS + RHS probe pairs; Chromium Next GEM Single Cell Fixed RNA Human Transcriptome Probe Set, PN-1000420/1000456). After hybridization, add Post-Hybridization (Post-Hyb) Buffer and wash to remove unbound probes. Recount fixed nuclei using Countess II FL with Ethidium Homodimer-1. Resuspend each hybridized sample in the volume specified for pooling.

steps.title: Pool up to 16 hybridized samples in equal proportions
steps.instruction: Pool up to 16 hybridized samples in equal proportions. Consult Pooling Workbook CG000565 for equalization strategies. Centrifuge pooled sample at 300 × g, 5 min, 4 °C. Resuspend in GEM loading volume specified in CG000527. Count pooled nuclei with Countess III FL. Adjust to target input concentration for Chromium Chip Q loading.

steps.title: Proceed immediately to GEM generation per CG000527 on Chromium X/iX
steps.instruction: Proceed immediately to GEM generation per CG000527 on Chromium X/iX. Construct Gene Expression libraries by PCR amplification using Dual Index Kit TT Set A. Assess library quality with Bioanalyzer (expected size ~200–300 bp). Sequence on an Illumina platform: 28 cycles (R1) + 10 cycles (i7) + 10 cycles (i5) + 90 cycles (R2). Target ≥ 15,000 reads per nucleus per sample.

steps.title: Raw sequencing data from the Chromium...
steps.instruction: Raw sequencing data from the Chromium Fixed RNA Profiling (Flex) libraries are processed using the cellranger multi pipeline — the only supported pipeline for Flex v1 multiplexed datasets. Analysis is performed via 10x Genomics Cloud Analysis (cloud.10xgenomics.com), a web-based platform that provides access to Cell Ranger pipelines. Upload FASTQ files using the Cloud CLI tool. After upload, FASTQ sets are automatically grouped. Assign the library type as "Flex Gene Expression" for each FASTQ set in the project interface before creating an analysis. Select all FASTQ files from a single GEM well and click "Create New Analysis". Specify the following Flex-specific parameters: (i) reference genome — human GRCh38-2024-A; (ii) probe set reference matching the kit used (PN-1000420 or PN-1000456); (iii) the Probe Barcode ID assigned to each sample during hybridization, for per-sample demultiplexing. Each GEM well constitutes one analysis. Cell Ranger multi automatically demultiplexes all pooled samples into per-sample outputs using the Probe Barcode sequences. The latest version of Cell Ranger is used by default on Cloud Analysis. Key outputs and quality control: web_summary.html — interactive per-sample QC report. Review: estimated number of nuclei, median genes per nucleus, sequencing saturation, fraction of reads confidently mapped to the probe set,and the knee plot for cell calling. Flag samples with saturation < 50% or median genes < 1,000 for re-sequencing. sample_cloupe.cloupe — Loupe Browser file for interactive visualization of UMAP projections, unsupervised clusters, and per-gene expression. Open in Loupe Browser (free desktop application,Windows/macOS) for exploratory cell type annotation without coding. filtered_feature_bc_matrix/ — per-sample feature-barcode count matrices (barcodes.tsv.gz, features.tsv.gz, matrix.mtx.gz). These are the primary input for downstream analysis in R(Seurat) or Python (Scanpy/AnnData).
